title: agropyron repens f. pectinatum
intro: Agropyron repens f. pectinatum, also known as creeping meadow grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is a perennial grass native to Europe and Western Asia, known for its medicinal properties and various health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Agropyron repens f. pectinatum can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
